title: A bhikkhu, bhikkhus, who has reverence, deference, and lives in concord with his fellow celibates — it is possible that he will fulfill the practice…
content: A bhikkhu, bhikkhus, who has reverence, deference, and lives in concord with his fellow celibates — it is possible that he will fulfill the practice of superior conduct. Having fulfilled the practice of superior conduct, it is possible that he will fulfill the learner's practice. Having fulfilled the learner's practice, it is possible that he will fulfill the rules of virtuous conduct. Having fulfilled the rules of virtuous conduct, it is possible that he will fulfill right view. Having fulfilled right view, it is possible that he will fulfill right concentration.

title: Elder Kātiyāna: O Kātiyāna, wake up, sit up! Do not sleep too much; awaken yourself! O heedless relatives, the passive, the lazy — Do not…
content:
Elder Kātiyāna:

O Kātiyāna, wake up, sit up!
Do not sleep too much; awaken yourself!
O heedless relatives, the passive, the lazy —
Do not let Death deceive and conquer you.

Like waves covering the great ocean,
So birth and old age cover and bury you.
Make yourself a safe island,
For no one else can serve and help you as a refuge.

The Teacher has laid down the path crossing beyond bonds,
Crossing beyond the fear of birth and old age.
In the evening and in the morning, live heedfully,
Attentive, persisting, in devoted effort.

Abandoning former bonds, wearing the outer robe,
Head shaven smooth, eating almsfood,
Do not delight in playing, do not indulge in sleeping.
Strive in meditation, O Kātiyāna!

Meditate, be victorious, O Kātiyāna!
Be skilled in the path, secure from bonds.
Attain supreme purity, you will attain Nibbāna,
Like water extinguishing fire.

Light is produced, the radiance is still weak,
Like a reed bent down by the wind.
O relative of Suda, do not grasp thus!
Expel Māra, dispassionate toward all feelings,
Wait for your time! Here you will be cooled.

title: Furthermore, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of…
content: Furthermore, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of suffering. For, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of suffering. Therefore, a disciple, having undertaken and trained in the training rules, should have unimpaired conduct, uncut conduct, unstained conduct, unblemished conduct in that training. Thus, bhikkhus, is the benefit of training.

title: Then the friends of the good man Raṭṭhapāla went to his parents and said: - Dear mother and father, the good man Raṭṭhapāla is lying…
content:
Then the friends of the good man Raṭṭhapāla went to his parents and said:
- Dear mother and father, the good man Raṭṭhapāla is lying on the bare ground and says: "Here I will die, or else I will go forth." If you do not give your permission for the good man Raṭṭhapāla to go forth from home to homelessness, he will die right there. But if you give your permission for him to go forth, you will be able to see him after he has gone forth. If the good man Raṭṭhapāla does not find delight in going forth, he will have no other way but to return here. Please give your permission for him to go forth.
- Dear children, we give our permission for the good man Raṭṭhapāla to go forth from home to homelessness, but after going forth, he must visit his parents.

title: - Bhikkhus, the arising, continuation, production, manifestation of the eye is the arising of suffering, the continuation of disease, the manifestation of aging and death.…
content: - Bhikkhus, the arising, continuation, production, manifestation of the eye is the arising of suffering, the continuation of disease, the manifestation of aging and death. The arising, continuation, production, manifestation of the ear ... of the nose ... of the tongue ... of the body ... of the mind is the arising of suffering, the continuation of disease, the manifestation of aging and death. The cessation, stilling, passing away of the eye is the cessation of suffering, the stilling of disease, the passing away of aging and death. The cessation, stilling, passing away of the ear ... nose ... tongue ... body ... mind is the cessation of suffering, the stilling of disease, the passing away of aging and death.

title: ... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One: A father delights in his son, A cow-owner…
content:
... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:
A father delights in his son,
A cow-owner delights in his cows,
A person with attachments delights,
One without attachments does not delight.
( Blessed One ):
A father grieves over his son,
A cow-owner grieves over his cows,
A person with attachments grieves,
One without attachments does not grieve.

title: Here, brahmin, a person is not dispassionate towards sensual pleasures, not free from desire, not free from affection, not free from thirst, not free from…
content: Here, brahmin, a person is not dispassionate towards sensual pleasures, not free from desire, not free from affection, not free from thirst, not free from fever, not free from craving. Then a severe illness arises in him. Affected by that severe illness, he reflects: 'Indeed, these delightful sensual pleasures will leave me,' or 'I will leave these delightful sensual pleasures.' He grieves, laments, weeps, beats his breast, and falls into confusion. This, brahmin, is a person who, by nature, being subject to death, is afraid and falls into terror when thinking of death.

title: Good, bhikkhus. I too have not seen nor heard, bhikkhus, that an ascetic or brahmin, dwelling devoted to the enjoyment of the pleasure of resting,…
content: Good, bhikkhus. I too have not seen nor heard, bhikkhus, that an ascetic or brahmin, dwelling devoted to the enjoyment of the pleasure of resting, the pleasure of lying down, the pleasure of sleeping, without guarding the sense doors, without moderation in eating, without devotion to wakefulness, without regard for wholesome states, without diligently developing the factors of enlightenment in the evening and in the morning, could, by the destruction of the taints, in this very life, with direct knowledge, realize, attain, and abide in the taintless liberation of mind and liberation by wisdom. Therefore, bhikkhus, you should train yourselves thus: 'With guarded sense doors, with moderation in eating, devoted to wakefulness, regarding wholesome states, diligently developing the factors of enlightenment in the evening and in the morning, we will, by the destruction of the taints, in this very life, with direct knowledge, realize, attain, and abide in the taintless liberation of mind and liberation by wisdom.' Thus, bhikkhus, you should train yourselves.

title: Feet painted with lac and ointment, Face powdered with white powder, Enough to fool a fool, But not one seeking the far shore.
content:
Feet painted with lac and ointment,
Face powdered with white powder,
Enough to fool a fool,
But not one seeking the far shore.

title: If an elder bhikkhu, Kassapa, delights in the training, praises undertaking the training, and encourages those bhikkhus who do not delight in the training, and…
content: If an elder bhikkhu, Kassapa, delights in the training, praises undertaking the training, and encourages those bhikkhus who do not delight in the training, and genuinely and timely praises those bhikkhus who delight in the training — such an elder bhikkhu, Kassapa, I praise. Why is that? Because other bhikkhus might associate with that elder bhikkhu, thinking: 'The Teacher praises him.' And those who associate with him might adopt his views. And those who adopt his views would bring about their own long-term welfare and happiness. Therefore, Kassapa, I praise such an elder bhikkhu. If a middle bhikkhu, Kassapa ... If a junior bhikkhu, Kassapa, delights in the training, praises undertaking the training, and encourages those bhikkhus who do not delight in the training, and genuinely and timely praises those bhikkhus who delight in the training — such a junior bhikkhu, Kassapa, I praise. Why is that? Because other bhikkhus might associate with that junior bhikkhu, thinking: 'The Teacher praises him.' And those who associate with him might adopt his views. And those who adopt his views would bring about their own long-term welfare and happiness. Therefore, Kassapa, I praise such a junior bhikkhu.
